data.title: Investigation Underway After Abnormal Token Minting on ZKSync Network
data.description:
According to PANews, an unusual minting of 110 million tokens has been detected on the ZKSync network, with approximately 66 million tokens already sold. Token unlock information indicates that the tokens belonging to the team and investors remain locked. The official response on Discord stated that the team has been notified and an investigation is ongoing.

No further official statements have been released at this time. Users are advised to closely monitor official channels and exercise caution in handling related token transactions.

data.title: Meme application chain Embr.fun completes $1.8 million in financing, led by Robot Ventures and others
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Embr.fun, a Meme application chain built on Initia, announced the completion of a $1.8 million financing round, led by Robot Ventures and Lattice, with TempleDAO and others participating. Embr.fun was built by Embr Labs and supported by the Initia Interwoven Stack.

data.title: Market News: ZKsync has sold 66 million tokens on the chain, and the team is investigating
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to market news: ZKsync has issued 110 million tokens on the chain and has been selling 66 million tokens on the chain. According to the token unlocking information, the team and investor tokens are still locked. According to the official Discord reply, the team has been notified that it is still under investigation.

data.title: BTC breaks through 86,000 USDT, up 1.71% in 24 hours
data.description: OKX market shows that BTC has broken through 86,000 USDT and is now at 86,005.2 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 1.71%. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Ethereum Ecosystem Generates Over $1 Billion in Q1 2025
data.description: According to PANews, data from Token Terminal reveals that the Ethereum ecosystem's applications generated approximately $1.01 billion in fee revenue during the first quarter of 2025. The primary contributors to this "on-chain GDP" were Lido Finance, Uniswap, and Aave.

data.title: Canada Temporarily Waives Tariffs on U.S. Manufacturing Imports
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Canadian Department of Finance has announced a temporary six-month exemption from tariffs on goods imported from the United States. This exemption applies to items used in manufacturing, processing, and food and beverage packaging sectors. The decision aims to support these industries by reducing costs associated with importing essential materials.

data.title: Sol Strategies and Pudgy Penguins Launch Solana Validator
data.description: According to Odaily, Sol Strategies has announced a collaboration with Pudgy Penguins to launch a dedicated Solana validator to support the PENGU ecosystem. The PENGU validator is now operational, forming part of SOL Strategies' white-label validator program, which offers secure and high-performance validation infrastructure. This partnership marks Pudgy Penguins' continued expansion into the Solana ecosystem.

data.title: Sol Strategies Partners with Pudgy Penguins to Launch PENGU Validator on Solana Chain
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, Sol Strategies, the "Solana version of MicroStrategy", announced a partnership with Pudgy Penguins to launch a dedicated Solana validator to support the PENGU ecosystem. It is reported that the PENGU validator is now in operation, providing a secure and high-performance verification infrastructure as part of the SOL Strategies white label validator program. This cooperation is also a continuation of Pudgy Penguins' expansion into the Solana ecosystem.

data.title: Phishing-as-a-service provider LabHost founder sentenced to eight and a half years in prison
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Zak Coyn, founder of LabHost, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) provider, was sentenced to eight and a half years in prison. According to the British Crown Prosecution Service, the 24-year-old earned $230,000 worth of cryptocurrency for designing and operating the website. These digital assets were laundered through multiple wallets and eventually converted into cash by a third party and transferred to his bank. It is estimated that thousands of criminals in 91 countries used LabHost to commit fraud, defrauding a total of more than one million people. (Decrypt)

data.title: Crypto’s debanking problem persists despite new regulations
data.description:
The crypto industry’s inability to access banking services still concerns many industry observers despite recent policy victories. In past years, financial services firms and banks concerned about fiduciary risk, reporting liabilities and reputational risk often would refuse to offer service to crypto firms — i. e. , “debanking” them
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-debanking-problem-persists-despite-new-regulations?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
